name: Arcade Games
description: Quarter-eating classics from Pong and Pac-Man to Street Fighter II.

questions.q: What 1972 Atari title is widely considered the first commercially successful arcade video game?

source.notes: Designed by Allan Alcorn under Nolan Bushnell.

questions.q: Which 1978 arcade game was so popular in Japan it was rumored to have caused a 100-yen coin shortage?

source.notes: Designed by Tomohiro Nishikado for Taito.

questions.q: Pac-Man was originally called what name in Japan, before localization changed it to avoid vandalism?

source.notes: Localizers worried U.S. arcades would have the 'P' scratched into an 'F'.

questions.q: In what year did Pac-Man first appear in arcades?

source.notes: Released by Namco in Japan in May 1980; Midway brought it to the U.S. that October.

questions.q: In which 1981 Nintendo arcade game did Mario first appear, originally as 'Jumpman'?
Which 1981 arcade game became the first to feature a female lead character?

source.notes: Originally an unauthorized hack called 'Crazy Otto' before Bally Midway licensed it.

questions.q: In which year did Atari's 'Asteroids' arrive in arcades?

source.notes: Used vector graphics and was Atari's best-selling arcade game ever.

questions.q: Which 1980 arcade game popularized the trackball as a controller input?

source.notes: Centipede's iconic gameplay was driven by its trackball; designed by Dona Bailey and Ed Logg.

questions.q: Which Capcom 1991 arcade game popularized 'Hadouken!' and turned 1-on-1 fighters into a global phenomenon?
Which Williams arcade game from 1982 has players riding ostriches in jousting battles?
Which 1982 Gottlieb arcade game stars an orange creature who hops on isometric cubes and curses in word balloons?

source.notes: Q*bert's 'curse' is the iconic '@!#?@!' speech bubble.

questions.q: Which 1981 sequel to Galaxian features dual fighter docking and dive-bombing alien insects?
Which Atari 2600 home title is famously blamed for the 1983 video game crash?

source.notes: Atari's port of the Pac-Man arcade game on 2600 was equally panned. Both were buried in a New Mexico landfill — later excavated in 2014.

questions.q: Which 1985 four-player co-op dungeon-crawler popularized the line 'Wizard needs food badly!'?

source.notes: Atari Games. Players chose Warrior, Valkyrie, Wizard, or Elf.

questions.q: Which 1986 Sega arcade racer puts the player in a Ferrari Testarossa cruising California highways?

source.notes: Designed by Yu Suzuki; the Ferrari is unlicensed but unmistakable.

questions.q: Which 1989 Sega arcade game lets you choose between a Dwarf, Barbarian, and Amazon to fight skeletons and dragons?
In Nintendo's 'Punch-Out!!' which French boxer is the first opponent and a frequent comic punching bag?
Who created the puzzle game Tetris in 1984?

source.notes: Pajitnov created Tetris in 1984 at the Soviet Academy of Sciences. Atari released the arcade version in 1988.

questions.q: Which 1995 Namco light-gun arcade game introduced the foot-pedal cover mechanic?

source.notes: Players step on a pedal to pop out from cover and shoot, then release to duck.

questions.q: Which 1999 Sega arcade game has players driving a yellow taxi around San Francisco-inspired streets?

source.notes: Soundtrack featured The Offspring and Bad Religion.
